response.justification: Bogdan is a strong full-stack and systems engineer with genuine geospatial and distributed systems experience, but the role's core requirement — production-grade C++ or Rust in a routing/navigation data pipeline context — is not met. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis project; his current and recent work is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js. There is no evidence of experience with routing tile generation, map data transformation pipelines, or navigation accuracy systems at scale.
strengths: Hands-on GIS/geospatial platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map) demonstrates domain familiarity with map data and spatial data collection
Distributed systems background at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O with DLang) shows comfort with high-throughput, performance-sensitive pipelines
Broad cross-stack and cross-language versatility (DLang, C++, TypeScript, Swift, Java) suggests ability to pick up new technologies quickly
concerns: C++ experience is shallow and over a decade old; no Rust experience listed at all — the role's primary language requirement is effectively unmet
No experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance pipelines, traffic signal integration, or map data freshness systems — the core domain of the role
Current trajectory (Naver: TypeScript, Node.js, AI-agent tooling) points away from systems programming and geospatial pipelines, not toward them
